reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output document is missing a vast majority of its content. Out of 27 pages in the source PDF, the output only provides content for pages 1-4, 24, 26, and 27. The JSON explicitly notes that 20 sections were omitted for token budget. This represents a total loss of roughly 75% of the source document's pages.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains 27 pages of content, including maps (p. 5), household connectivity infographics (p. 6-9), strategic vision (p. 14), the state broadband plan (p. 16), and specific program details.
concerns.output_quote: The output JSON contains an explicit omission note: '{"_omitted": "20 sections omitted for token budget"}'. It jumps from page 4 (Typical Broadband Usage) directly to page 24 (GREAT Grant Program).
concerns.location: Sections list, between reading_order index 19 and 111

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There are several instances of significant text omission within the sections that were converted. On page 2, the descriptive sub-bullets for every technology type (DSL, Cable, Fiber, Wireless, Satellite) containing speed ranges and technical specifications are completely missing. On page 4, the source URL is missing from the citation. On page 26, the contact email address is missing. On page 27, all social media handles and the primary department website URL are missing.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2: DSL sub-bullets ('Runs over traditional copper...', 'Speeds range from...'); Page 4: URL 'https://www.fcc.gov/research-reports/guides/household-broadband-guide'; Page 26: email 'firsttech@nc.gov'; Page 27: Handles like '@NCDIT', '@BroadbandIO' and URL 'it.nc.gov'.
concerns.output_quote: Page 2 list items (reading_order 8) are missing all sub-bullet text content. Page 4 'Source' (reading_order 19) is truncated. Page 26 (reading_order 122) ends before the email. Page 27 section contains no text for handles or URLs.

concerns.analysis: In Section 24 (Page 24), the converter interprets a two-column list of awardees as a table. While the table interpretation is logical, the first row of data ('Bertie' and 'Roanoke Connect Holdings') is misidentified as a header row ('is_header_row': true and 'cell_type': 'header'). These are data values belonging to the body of the table, not column headers.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list of awards on page 24 begins with 'Bertie' and 'Roanoke Connect Holdings' as the first entry, not as titles for the columns.
concerns.output_quote: The first row of the table in Section 24 (reading_order 114) is marked as a header row: "is_header_row": true, "cell_type": "header".
concerns.location: Section title 'GREAT Grant Program', Page 24, element index 114

concerns.analysis: Numerous meaningful and content-critical images are missing from the HTML output. This includes data maps (pages 5, 11, 19), house grid data representations (pages 6-9), conceptual hexagon diagrams (pages 10, 12), website navigation graphics (page 21), and the report cover photo (page 22). In many cases, these images are replaced by their alt-text descriptions (e.g., 'A pattern of houses icons', 'Two blue hexagon shapes...', 'Abstract colorful wavy background...'), which confirms the images them
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 5: 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory' Map; Pages 6-9: House icon grids; Page 11: '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates' Map; Page 19: 'Technical Assistance Regions' Map; Page 21: 'The Playbook' navigation images; Page 22: Photo of children with tablets.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output shows text placeholders or alt-text descriptions instead of these images. For example, 'A pattern of houses icons.' appears instead of the page 6-9 graphics, and descriptive text appears where the maps and diagrams should be.

concerns.analysis: On Page 2, the source document contains an unordered list where several items (e.g., 'Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)') have nested sub-bullets describing speeds and technical details. The JSON conversion (reading_order 8) renders these as a flat list and entirely omits the nested sub-bullet content, failing to preserve the structural nesting and meaning of the list.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)
- Runs over traditional copper phone lines...
- Speeds range from 1.5 Mbps to 80 Mbps
concerns.output_quote: The list item for DSL (reading_order 8) contains no nested content: "nested_list": null.
concerns.location: Section title 'Broadband Technology Types', Page 2, element index 8

concerns.analysis: Multiple URLs and contact email addresses are clearly visible in the source document (Pages 3, 4, 16, 26, 27). In the JSON output, these are rendered as plain text within paragraphs but are not hyperlinked or identified as link elements, making them non-functional for accessibility purposes.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: URLs on pages 3, 4, 16, 27 and email address 'firsttech@nc.gov' on page 26.
concerns.output_quote: The contact info and URLs are present only as text strings, such as in reading_order 122: "Red Grasso, Director/FirstNet SPOC; Charles Laird, Program Specialist firsttech@nc.gov".
concerns.location: Various pages including Page 3 (index 14), Page 4 (index 19), and Page 26 (index 122).

action_items: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.
